info.title: Pipecat Cloud
info.description: REST API for Pipecat Cloud. The public API (`/v1/public`, authenticated with a public API key) starts agent sessions and proxies requests to them. The private API (`/v1`, authenticated with a private API key) manages agents, secrets, builds, regions, and organization settings.

400.description: Invalid request or agent already exists

value.error: Service already exists
Secret set 'my-secrets' is in region 'us-east' but service will be created in region 'us-west'. Secrets must be in the same region as the service.
Image pull secret set 'dockerhub-creds' is in region 'us-east' but service will be created in region 'us-west'. Image pull secrets must be in the same region as the service.
Either image or buildId is required for service creation
Cannot specify both image and buildId. Use one or the other.
Build not found
Build is in region 'eu-central' but service is in region 'us-west'. Builds must be in the same region as the service.
Build is not ready (status: building). Only successful builds can be deployed.

404.description: Secret set or image pull secret set not found

parameters.name: includeActiveDeployment

parameters.name: region

Cannot specify both image and buildId. Use one or the other.
Build not found
Build is in region 'eu-central' but service is in region 'us-west'. Builds must be in the same region as the service.
Build is not ready (status: building). Only successful builds can be deployed.

get.description: Retrieves logs for the specified agent with optional filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return

parameters.description: Number of log entries to skip

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Free-text search query to filter logs

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order for logs

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Filter logs to those after this Unix timestamp

parameters.name: endTime

relation.description: Relation type for the total count (eq = exact, gte = greater than or equal)

post.description: Create a new build from an uploaded context. If a successful build already exists with the same context hash, region, and Dockerfile path, the cached build is returned instead of triggering a new build.

200.description: Cached build found - no new build triggered

400.description: Invalid request (e.g., upload not found, cloud build not enabled)

get.description: Retrieve the current values of all configurable properties for your organization. Returns only public (user-configurable) properties without schema metadata. For schema information including available values and constraints, use the `/properties/schema` endpoint.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

patch.description: Update one or more configurable properties for your organization. Only public (user-configurable) properties can be modified via this endpoint. Properties are validated against their schema and constraints before being applied. Returns the complete property schema including metadata for all properties.

defaultRegion.description: Default region for new service deployments. Must be one of the [available regions](/pipecat-cloud/rest-reference/endpoint/regions-list) for your organization.
Default region for new service deployments

400.description: Bad request - Invalid property name, value, or validation error

value.error: Property 'invalidProp' is not a valid property
Region 'invalid-region' is not available. Available regions: us-west, us-east, eu-central
No properties provided

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Retrieve metadata about all configurable properties for your organization, including type information, constraints, current values, default values, and dynamically available values (e.g., available regions).

defaultRegion.description: Default region for new service deployments

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Retrieve a list of all regions available for deploying agents and storing secrets. This endpoint can be accessed using either Clerk authentication or a Private API key.

put.summary: Create or update a secret set
put.description:
Create a new secret set or update an existing one. Supports both regular secrets (multiple key-value pairs) and image pull secrets.

Provisioning is **asynchronous**: the response is `202 Accepted` once the values have been stored, and the secret set is then provisioned into the target region in the background. Use `GET /secrets/{setName}` to observe the readiness status (`pending` → `ready`). Deploys that bind a secret set whose status is not `ready` are rejected with `409 Conflict`.

parameters.name: setName
parameters.description: Name of the secret set to create or update

regularSecrets.description: Create or update a set with multiple key-value pairs

imagePullSecret.description: Create or update an image pull secret

202.description: Secret set accepted. Values have been stored and the set is being provisioned into the target region in the background. Poll `GET /secrets/{setName}` until `status` is `ready` before binding it to a deploy.

region.description: The region where the secret set was created or exists

400.description: Bad request - Invalid parameters or operation not allowed

value.error: Must provide isImagePullSecret, host, and secretValue to update an image pull secret
Cannot update existing regular secrets to image pull secrets
Secret already exists in region 'us-west'. Cannot change region to 'us-east'.
Invalid region or organization does not have access
Invalid request format

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

409.description: Conflict - Another request is modifying this secret set concurrently. Retry the request.

example.error: Secret set 'my-secrets' was modified concurrently. Please retry.

delete.description: Permanently removes a secret set and all its associated secrets.

parameters.name: setName
parameters.description: Name of the secret set to delete

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Retrieve the keys (not values) of a specific secret set. Image pull secrets cannot be retrieved.

parameters.name: setName
parameters.description: Name of the secret set to retrieve

200.description: Secret set details, including readiness status

region.description: The region where the secret set is stored

status.description: Readiness state. `pending` while provisioning is in flight, `ready` once the set can be bound to a deploy, `failed` if provisioning could not complete.

errorMessage.description: Customer-facing message returned only when `status` is `failed`. Generic by design — internal diagnostic details are kept in platform logs.

400.description: Bad request - Cannot retrieve image pull secrets

example.error: Cannot retrieve image pull secrets by name

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

delete.summary: Delete a specific secret from a set
delete.description: Remove a specific secret key from a secret set. Cannot be used with image pull secrets.

parameters.name: setName
parameters.description: Name of the secret set

parameters.name: secretKey
parameters.description: Name of the secret key to delete

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Retrieve a list of all secret sets for the organization.

get.description: Proxies a GET request to an endpoint defined in your running bot. The response is returned directly from the bot.

post.description: Proxies a POST request to an endpoint defined in your running bot. The request body and response are passed through directly.

requestBody.description: Request body to pass to the bot endpoint

schema.description: Arbitrary JSON data to send to your bot endpoint

put.description: Proxies a PUT request to an endpoint defined in your running bot.

requestBody.description: Request body to pass to the bot endpoint

schema.description: Arbitrary JSON data to send to your bot endpoint

patch.description: Proxies a PATCH request to an endpoint defined in your running bot.

requestBody.description: Request body to pass to the bot endpoint

schema.description: Arbitrary JSON data to send to your bot endpoint

delete.description: Proxies a DELETE request to an endpoint defined in your running bot.

options.description: Proxies an OPTIONS request to an endpoint defined in your running bot.

head.description: Proxies a HEAD request to an endpoint defined in your running bot.

parameters.name: agentName
parameters.description: Name of the agent to start

PrivateKeyAuth.description:
Authentication requires a Pipecat Cloud Private API token.

Generate a Private API key from your Dashboard (Settings > API Keys > Private > Create key) and include it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

BearerAuth.description: Authentication using a Pipecat Cloud API token.

PublicKeyAuth.description:
Authentication using a Pipecat Cloud public API key.

Generate a public API key from your Dashboard (Settings > API Keys > Public > Create key) and include it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

serviceName.description:
Name of the agent to create.

Must start with a lowercase letter or number, can include hyphens, and must end with a lowercase letter or number. No uppercase letters or special characters allowed. Maximum 54 characters.

image.description: The container image to use for the agent. Either `image` or `buildId` must be provided, but not both.

buildId.description: The ID of a successful cloud build to deploy. Either `image` or `buildId` must be provided, but not both. The build must be in the same region as the agent.

region.description: The region where the agent will be deployed. If not specified, defaults to `us-west`. All secrets and image pull secrets referenced by this agent must be in the same region.

nodeType.description: The type of node to run the agent on. Only `arm` is supported at this time.

imagePullSecretSet.description: The name of the image pull secret set to use

secretSet.description: The name of the secret set to use

krispViva.description: Krisp VIVA noise cancellation configuration. [Learn more](/pipecat-cloud/guides/krisp-viva).

audioFilter.description:
The Krisp VIVA audio filter model to use:

- `tel`: Optimized for telephony, cellular, landline, mobile, desktop, and browser (up to 16kHz)
- `pro`: Optimized for mobile, desktop, and browser with WebRTC (up to 32kHz)
- `null`: Disables Krisp VIVA

By default, Pipecat processes input audio at 16kHz, making `tel` appropriate for most use cases.

agentProfile.description:
The agent profile to use for resource allocation. Valid values are:

- `agent-1x`: 0.5 vCPU and 1 GB of memory. Best for voice agents.
- `agent-2x`: 1 vCPU and 2 GB of memory. Well suited for voice and video agents or voice agents requiring extra processing.
- `agent-3x`: 1.5 vCPU and 3 GB of memory. Best for voice and video agents requiring extra processing or multiple video inputs.

maxSessionDuration.description: Maximum session duration in seconds. When a session reaches this limit, the agent's connection is forcibly closed — the session is cut off mid-flight with no notice to the bot. Valid range: 60 to 14400 (4 hours). Defaults to 7200 (2 hours) when unset.

deploymentID.description: ID of the deployment for this revision

readyReplicas.description: Number of ready replicas for this revision

phaseStartedAt.description: Timestamp when the current phase started

hasInfrastructureIssue.description: Set to true when an internal infrastructure component is experiencing issues. If absent, infrastructure is healthy. Contact support if this flag appears.

RevisionHealth.description: Health summary for the agent's code. Present once replicas have started running.

ready.description: Whether the agent code is passing readiness checks

state.description: Current state of the agent code

restartCount.description: Total number of restarts across all replicas

replicasStarted.description: Number of replicas that have been started (may or may not be ready)

reason.description: Reason for the current state (e.g., CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, OOMKilled, Error)

lastExitCode.description: Exit code from the most recent crash (e.g., 1 for application error, 137 for out-of-memory)

lastTerminationReason.description: Why the agent code last exited. When reason is CrashLoopBackOff, this reveals the underlying cause (e.g., OOMKilled, Error)

message.description: Detailed error output from the crash, such as a Python traceback. Captured from the agent's stderr/stdout on non-zero exit.

serviceId.description: ID of the service for this session

organizationId.description: ID of the organization that this session is associated with

deploymentId.description: ID of the deployment that this session is associated with

botStartSeconds.description: Number of seconds between bot start request and bot start

coldStart.description: Whether the bot start was a [cold start](/pipecat-cloud/fundamentals/scaling#cold-starts)

completionStatus.description: The completion status of the session. This can be an http return code or a string like `HTTP_COMPLETED`, `HTTP_ERROR`, `WS_CONNECTION_CLOSED`, among others

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the session was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the session was last updated

resourceMetrics.description: Resource usage metrics for the session, if available

sessionId.description: ID of the session these metrics belong to

cpuMillicoresP50.description: CPU usage at the 50th percentile (in millicores)

cpuMillicoresP90.description: CPU usage at the 90th percentile (in millicores)

cpuMillicoresP95.description: CPU usage at the 95th percentile (in millicores)

cpuMillicoresP99.description: CPU usage at the 99th percentile (in millicores)

memoryBytesP50.description: Memory usage at the 50th percentile (in bytes)

memoryBytesP90.description: Memory usage at the 90th percentile (in bytes)

memoryBytesP95.description: Memory usage at the 95th percentile (in bytes)

memoryBytesP99.description: Memory usage at the 99th percentile (in bytes)

eventTs.description: Timestamp of the most recent metrics event

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the metrics record was created

meetingIds.description: Daily meeting IDs associated with this session

region.description: The region where the agent is deployed

desiredDeploymentId.description: ID of the deployment that was requested. Use this to track which deployment should be running.

activeDeploymentId.description: **Deprecated**: Use `desiredDeploymentId` instead. ID of the requested deployment.

reconciledDeploymentId.description: ID of the deployment that the operator has actually reconciled. Compare with `desiredDeploymentId` to determine if a deployment update has been processed.

organizationId.description: Organization ID this agent belongs to

deletedAt.description: Deletion timestamp, if the agent has been deleted

deployment.description: Details of the active deployment (only included when includeActiveDeployment=true)

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp of the deployment

image.description: The container image to use for the agent. Cannot be specified together with `buildId`.

buildId.description: The ID of a successful cloud build to deploy. Cannot be specified together with `image`. The build must be in the same region as the agent.

nodeType.description: The type of node to run the agent on. Only `arm` is supported at this time.

imagePullSecretSet.description: The name of the image pull secret set to use

secretSet.description: The name of the secret set to use

krispViva.description: Krisp VIVA noise cancellation configuration. [Learn more](/pipecat-cloud/guides/krisp-viva).

audioFilter.description:
The Krisp VIVA audio filter model to use:

- `tel`: Optimized for telephony, cellular, landline, mobile, desktop, and browser (up to 16kHz)
- `pro`: Optimized for mobile, desktop, and browser with WebRTC (up to 32kHz)
- `null`: Disables Krisp VIVA

By default, Pipecat processes input audio at 16kHz, making `tel` appropriate for most use cases.

agentProfile.description:
The agent profile to use for resource allocation. Valid values are:

- `agent-1x`: 0.5 vCPU and 1 GB of memory. Best for voice agents.
- `agent-2x`: 1 vCPU and 2 GB of memory. Well suited for voice and video agents or voice agents requiring extra processing.
- `agent-3x`: 1.5 vCPU and 3 GB of memory. Best for voice and video agents requiring extra processing or multiple video inputs.

forceRedeploy.description: Force a new deployment even if the configuration hasn't changed. Useful for picking up updated container images when using mutable tags like `latest`, or for refreshing modified secret values.

maxSessionDuration.description: Maximum session duration in seconds. When a session reaches this limit, the agent's connection is forcibly closed — the session is cut off mid-flight with no notice to the bot. Valid range: 60 to 14400 (4 hours). Defaults to 7200 (2 hours) when unset.

region.description: The region where the agent is deployed

ready.description: Whether the latest deployment has passed validation and is ready. For scale-to-zero agents, this is true even when scaled down to zero replicas.

available.description: Whether the agent can accept new sessions. True when any pods are ready to handle traffic, or when scaled to zero but healthy (requests will queue and trigger scale-up). During rolling updates, this may be true even if `ready` is false, as old pods continue serving while new ones validate.

desiredDeploymentId.description: ID of the deployment that was requested. Use this to track which deployment should be running.

activeDeploymentId.description: **Deprecated**: Use `desiredDeploymentId` instead. ID of the requested deployment.

reconciledDeploymentId.description: ID of the deployment that the operator has actually reconciled. Compare with `desiredDeploymentId` to determine if a deployment update has been processed. When these match and `ready` is true, the deployment is fully complete.

activeDeploymentReady.description: **Deprecated**: Use `ready` instead. Whether the active deployment is ready. This field is redundant with `ready` and will be removed in a future version.

dailyNodeType.description: Type of node to run on

image.description: Container image used. Redacted when deployed via a build.

imagePullSecretName.description: Name of the image pull secret used

envFromSecretNames.description: Names of secrets to use as environment variables

audioFilters.description: Whether Krisp VIVA audio filters are enabled

maxSessionDurationSeconds.description: Maximum session duration in seconds configured for this service. Absent when the service is using the platform default.

serviceId.description: ID of the service this deployment belongs to

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp of the deployment

agentProfile.description: The agent profile used for resource allocation

audioFilter.description: The currently configured Krisp VIVA audio filter model (tel, pro, or null if disabled)

currentRevision.description: Status of the current (latest) deployment revision

previousRevision.description: Status of the previous deployment revision (present during rolling updates)

uploadId.description: The upload ID returned from the upload-url endpoint.

region.description: Target region for the build. Must match the region used when getting the upload URL.

dockerfilePath.description: Path to the Dockerfile within the context archive.

contextHash.description: Hash of the uploaded context. Used for build caching.

cached.description: Whether this response uses a cached build (true) or triggered a new build (false).

dockerfilePath.description: Path to the Dockerfile used for this build.

imageUri.description: URI of the built image. Use this value when deploying an agent. Only present when status is 'success'.

logsUrl.description: URL to view build logs in the cloud console.

errorMessage.description: Detailed error message if the build failed. Includes information from the build phases to help diagnose issues.
errorMessage.example: Docker build failed: COPY failed - file not found: requirements.txt

contextSizeBytes.description: Size of the uploaded context in bytes.

imageSizeBytes.description: Size of the built Docker image in bytes. Only present for successful builds.

buildDurationSeconds.description: Total build duration in seconds. Only present when build is complete.

completedAt.description: When the build completed (success, failed, or timeout).

createdAt.description: When the build record was created.

updatedAt.description: When the build record was last updated.

logs.description: Array of log lines from the build process. May be empty if the build hasn't started generating logs yet.

nextToken.description: Pagination token for retrieving additional logs. Present when more logs are available.

total.description: Total number of builds matching the filters.

region.description: Target region for the build. Must be a region where cloud builds are enabled.

uploadId.description: Unique identifier for this upload. Use this when creating the build.

uploadUrl.description: Pre-signed URL to upload your context archive. Use HTTP POST with form-data.

uploadFields.description: Form fields that must be included when uploading to the pre-signed URL.

expiresAt.description: When the upload URL expires. Upload must complete before this time.

defaultRegion.description: Default region for new service deployments

PropertySchema.description: Schema information for a single property

type.description: Property data type (string, number, boolean, array, etc.)

description.example: Default region for new service deployments

default.description: Default value when property is not set

currentValue.description: Current value of the property for this organization

readOnly.description: Whether this property is read-only (cannot be modified via API)

availableValues.description: List of valid values for this property (if applicable)

region.description: The region where the secret set will be created. If not specified, defaults to the organization's default region. Secrets must be in the same region as the agents that use them. Cannot be changed after creation.

isImagePullSecret.description: Must be true for image pull secrets

host.description: Host URL. A trailing slash will be added automatically if not present.

region.description: The region where the secret set will be created. If not specified, defaults to the organization's default region. Secrets must be in the same region as the agents that use them.
The region where the secret set is stored

status.description: Readiness state. `pending` while provisioning is in flight, `ready` once the set can be bound to a deploy, `failed` if provisioning could not complete.

errorMessage.description: Customer-facing message returned only when `status` is `failed`.

fieldName.description: Name of the secret key (values are not returned for security)

ProxiedResponse.description: Response from your bot endpoint. The schema depends on what your bot returns.

error.description: Error message describing what went wrong

code.description: Error code for programmatic handling. Deployment-related codes (PCC-*) are documented at https://docs.pipecat.ai/pipecat-cloud/fundamentals/error-codes

createDailyRoom.description: Whether to create a Daily room for the service instance

dailyRoomProperties.description:
Optional configuration for the Daily room. Only used when createDailyRoom is true.

See [Daily API documentation](https://docs.daily.co/reference/rest-api/rooms/config) for supported properties.

dailyMeetingTokenProperties.description:
Optional configuration for the Daily meeting token. Only used when createDailyRoom is true.

See [Daily API documentation](https://docs.daily.co/reference/rest-api/meeting-tokens/config) for supported properties.

enableDefaultIceServers.description: Whether to use the default STUN/TURN ICE servers provided by Pipecat Cloud.

transport.description: Explicitly specify which transport the bot should use.

body.description: Arbitrary user data / configuration object to pass to the service instance. Accessible as the first parameter of the `bot` method or custom entry point. Maximum size: 1 MB for `transport: "daily"` or `"webrtc"`, 4 KB for `"websocket"`. For `transport: "websocket"`, the body is returned in the response's `body` field base64-encoded; the service must be deployed with `websocket_auth = "token"` to use this encoding convenience. See the [`Starting Sessions` docs](/pipecat-cloud/fundamentals/active-sessions#passing-data) for details.

token.description: HMAC session token for WebSocket authentication (only when transport is "websocket")

wsUrl.description: WebSocket URL to connect to (only when transport is "websocket")

body.description: Base64-encoded JSON body for the bot (only when transport is "websocket" and the request included a `body`). Append to `wsUrl` as a `?body=` query parameter when connecting.

dailyRoom.description: URL of the created Daily room (if createDailyRoom was set to true)

dailyToken.description: Token to access the Daily room (if createDailyRoom was set to true)

sessionId.description: Session ID for the created session

iceConfig.description: ICE server configuration (only included when enableDefaultIceServers is true)

session_id.description: Session ID of the stopped session

SessionId.name: sessionId
SessionId.description: Session ID returned from the start endpoint

Path.name: path
Path.description: The endpoint path defined in your bot using the `@app` decorator

example.error: Attempt to start agent when deployment is not in ready state. Is your image pull secret valid?
Internal server error. Please check logs for more information or contact support.
